RU.operation_name: Pravda Network — Global Content Laundering Infrastructure
RU.target_audience: 110+ countries; primary focus France, Germany, Ukraine, Moldova, Serbia
RU.summary: DFRLab/CheckFirst April 2025 investigation: 3.7 million articles published; linked to TigerWeb (Crimea-based IT firm) and its owner. Top sources: TASS (136K), RIA Novosti (99K), Lenta (89K), Komsomolskaya Pravda (59K), RT (54K). Primary focus: France (394K articles), Germany (377K), Ukraine (270K), Moldova (245K), Serbia (229K). Domains surged pre-2024 EU elections and Oct 2024–Jan 2025. Real-time dashboard released April 2025.

RU.posture: Sustained and escalating FIMI operations targeting EU election cycles (Hungary April 2026, Czech October 2025, Moldova September 2025). AI-enabled content generation (Doppelganger Gen 3, Operation Overload, Pravda Network) fully operational. Recorded Future assesses Russia likely moving toward New Generation Warfare campaign posture in 2026–2028 window.
RU.doctrine_note: No doctrine shift observed. Russian FIMI doctrine continues to emphasize synthetic content generation, cross-platform amplification via inauthentic accounts, narrative persistence across EU information space, and exploitation of democratic election cycles. AI-FIMI integration fully mature.

CN.doctrine_summary: Discourse Power (话语权) / United Front Work / Three Warfares (心理战·舆论战·法律战). Three Warfares concept formalised 2003 PLA Political Work Regulations. Strategic patience: long-term narrative shaping over episodic operations.
primary_infrastructure: Dragonbridge/Spamouflage (Ministry of Public Security '912 Special Project')

CN.posture: Sustained high-tempo IO operations. Dragonbridge confirmed as most prolific PRC-linked IO operator globally. Expanding assertiveness in Asia-Pacific. AI experimentation ongoing but engagement remains low. Military-Civil Fusion framework provides structural capability development pathway.
CN.doctrine_note: No doctrine shift. Chinese FIMI doctrine continues to emphasize long-term narrative positioning, influencer co-option (borrowed mouths strategy), and state media amplification. Spamouflage/Dragonbridge operational tempo high but effectiveness limited outside Chinese-language information spaces.

IR.doctrine_summary: IRGC cognitive warfare / information operations. Elevated operational tempo since October 7 2023. Blurs distinction between genuine political expression and foreign-directed amplification — most methodologically difficult attribution category. EU designated IRGC as terrorist organisation January 29, 2026.

attribution_evidence_chains.finding: Iranian CIB (STORM-2035 linked) targeting Azeri audiences; female journalist persona impersonation; Google Threat Intelligence Group aided takedown
EU unanimously designated IRGC as terrorist organisation; asset freezes and travel restrictions imposed; constrains IRGC IO financial infrastructure in Europe

EU terrorist designation adds financial constraint. Iranian IO doctrine continues to emphasize asymmetric narrative operations exploiting societal divisions.

GULF.doctrine_summary: Strategic narrative management / lobbying-to-influence. UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar operating with distinct doctrines. UAE: targeted intelligence operations (Project Raven, NSO-client). Saudi: narrative seeding and anti-rival campaigns. Qatar: Al Jazeera soft power and parliamentary lobbying.
primary_infrastructure: Qatar: Al Jazeera, European parliamentary channels

US.doctrine_summary: Near-state political influence operations / PAC-adjacent activity. Methodological note: this monitor applies the same inclusion criteria to US operations as to other actors — cross-border reach using covert means, foreign funding, or coordinated platform manipulation. Domestic political communication without foreign dimension is outside scope.

ai_fimi_calibration_2026.note: AI-generated FIMI is now operationally embedded across all actor categories. Detection of AI-generated content is not automatically a new finding — assess departure from baseline.
ai_fimi_calibration_2026.eeas_4th_report_finding: AI involved in >25% of 540 FIMI incidents documented in 2025 (EEAS 4th Annual Report, March 2026). AI-generated text, synthetic audio, and manipulated video shifted from experimental use to routine deployment.
ai_fimi_calibration_2026.deepfake_scale: Estimated 8 million deepfake videos shared online by 2025 (EPRS estimate); 179 reported deepfake incidents in Q1 2025 alone — surpassing full-year 2024 total (Cyble)
